content.full_text:
GORDON P. LEVY
ATTORNEY AT LAW
36620 GEIGER AVENUE
PALMDALE, CALIFORNIA 93551
(805) 273-5031

Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
428 J. Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA. 95814                July 25, 1990

Subject: Consultant Technical Assistance

Gentlemen;

As you know, I recently ran for the Republican nomination for Secretary of State. My Campaign bank account is now flat, or at the very most has about $150.00 in it. No more contributions are expected. Last week I felt sorry for a friend of mine who belongs to the same church I do. You see, he owns a Print-Pro shop and had sold me several thousand brochures near the end of the campaign, and I still owed him about $700.00 from the campaign but there was no money left to pay him. I handed him a personal check at church, for $500., which happened to be in my personal account because of a change of plans. The $500 check was drawn on my personal account. In fact, I would like to pay him the rest soon from my personal funds.

My campaign treasurer, Jim Bruns, now lives in far-off Carpenteria, California, and I have trouble reaching him even by telephone. The campaign bank account in San Fernando, California, does not allow for me to write checks -- only James Bruns can write checks on the campaign account. Therefore, it would be a risky business, at best, for me to place any of my personal funds in that account so that checks could be written on campaign account checks (if I had any, which I don't).

Any advice?

Sincerely,

[Signature]
GORDON LEVY
home: (805) 273-5031
work: (818) 898-2509
